retry_reason: Failure means the refcount is zero (object is dying/dead); retrying will not revive the object.
contract_summary: Conditional get-wrapper: acquires a reference on a btrfs_root object only if its refcount is still non-zero (object alive). Returns the root pointer on success, NULL if the object is already being freed. Caller must check the return value and later release the reference with btrfs_put_root (or equivalent). Typically used to safely obtain a reference from an RCU-protected or otherwise unstable pointer, ensuring the object survives during use.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to conditionally increment the refcount of a btrfs_root via refcount_inc_not_zero. Only additional logic is a NULL pointer check.

check.reason: The function uses refcount_inc_not_zero(&root->refs), which increments if non-zero and returns root. The access path from the returned pointer (same as input root) to the atomic_t is root.refs.refs, matching the callee's path (r.refs). Type chain btrfs_root->refcount_t->atomic_t is correct. Segment counts match (3 each). Location ('return', index 0) correctly identifies the object passed to the caller.
